sections.paragraph:
τ´ numerical sign = 300 (τριακόσιοι; SibOr 5, 21; 38; 42). Because of its form (T) a symbol of the cross B 9:8 (cp. Lucian s.v. σταυρός, end), spelled out ταῦ, v.l.

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 987
